page: Vultr vbm-8c-64gb-amd is a bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal NVMe family. It is equipped with an AMD EPYC 4345P processor built on the x86_64 architecture, featuring 8 physical cores and 16 vCPUs with 2 threads per core. The dedicated CPU operates at a base clock speed of 3.9 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. The system is provisioned with 64.0 GB of memory, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with a local 3890 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top tier. Passmark memory score also registers in the top tier. Conversely, multi-core performance metrics, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, fall into the average category. Memory bandwidth tests show average read-only performance for both small and large blocks, though large block write bandwidth is positioned in the weak category. Static web serving throughput is strong, placing in the top 25%.

Given the combination of high-capacity local NVMe storage and top-tier single-core CPU performance, this bare metal server is well-suited for database hosting, static web serving, and single-threaded data processing tasks. The dedicated hardware allocation ensures consistent performance without hypervisor overhead. From a resource density perspective, the inclusion of a large 3890 GB NVMe SSD alongside 64 GB of RAM offers a cost-efficient profile for storage-heavy workloads that do not require massive multi-threaded parallelization.
description: Vultr vbm-8c-64gb-amd is an x86_64 bare metal server powered by an 8-core, 16-vCPU AMD EPYC 4345P processor running at 3.9 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of memory and a substantial 3890 GB local NVMe SSD. While its multi-core benchmarks and memory write bandwidth are average to weak, it delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and memory scores. This hardware configuration, combined with strong static web serving throughput, makes it highly suitable for database management, web serving, and single-threaded processing workloads requiring dedicated resources without virtualization overhead.
og_description: Vultr vbm-8c-64gb-amd is an x86_64 bare metal server featuring an 8-core AMD EPYC 4345P CPU, 64 GB RAM, and a 3890 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vbm-8c-64gb-amd is a bare metal server with an AMD EPYC 4345P CPU, 64 GB RAM, and 3890 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for database and web workloads.
tagline: A dedicated bare metal server combining top-tier single-core CPU performance with high-capacity local NVMe storage for database and web workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory and 3890 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
